completedPhaseClaimDetails.summary: Created BUG-016-W4 packet under specs/016-weather-connector/bugs after the stabilize trigger probe in sweep-2026-05-23-r30 round 26 identified a cache-saturation defect in internal/connector/weather/weather.go. Authored bug.md classifying the finding as weather-connector owned and assigning Finding IDs F1 (production stability defect) and F2 (test regression that codified the broken policy).

Root cause triage recorded in design.md: historical archive entries are inserted into the shared 1024-entry cache with a 100-year TTL, and evictExpiredLocked only removed entries whose expiresAt is in the past. Once 1024+ historical lookups accumulate, every subsequent current/forecast insertion is silently dropped with 'weather cache full, discarding new entry' and the cross-Sync cache benefit is lost. Selected design: rename to evictOneLocked, evict one entry per call, prefer expired entries, fall back to the entry with the latest expiresAt.

Authored design.md with Current Truth at HEAD before BUG-016-W4, root cause, three design decisions (single-cache preserved, latest-expiresAt fallback, evict-one-per-call), selected design code, test plan, change boundary, and adversarial fidelity proof plan. spec.md and scopes.md authored alongside; scenario-manifest.json mapped SCN-BUG016W4-001..005 to linked unit tests and evidence refs.

Applied production fix to internal/connector/weather/weather.go: renamed evictExpiredLocked to evictOneLocked at line 824, added the latest-expiresAt fallback eviction branch with explicit single-entry semantics, and updated all three production callers (decodeCurrent line 603, decodeForecast line 706, decodeHistorical line 812). Code Diff Evidence recorded in report.md under '## Code Diff Evidence' / '### Code Diff Evidence'.

Test-owned evidence captured: renamed TestEvictExpiredLocked to TestEvictOneLocked_PrefersExpired (single-entry assertion); updated TestCacheOverflow_AllValid to assert the new policy (overflow inserted, longest-expiry evicted, cap preserved); added TestEviction_HistoricalDoesNotStarveEphemeral as the primary adversarial regression; added TestEviction_LongestExpiryEvictedWhenFull as the eviction-policy invariant. Targeted 'go test -race -count=1 -v' PASS evidence recorded in report.md under '## Test Evidence' / '### Test Evidence'.

Adversarial fidelity proven by revert-and-restore cycle: temporarily reverted the latest-expiresAt fallback branch in internal/connector/weather/weather.go via in-place scratch mutation; observed all three affected tests FAIL with their concrete BUG-016-W4 diagnostic strings (current key missing, historical eviction count wrong, cache size 3 instead of 2); restored the fix from /tmp/weather-fixed.go and verified bit-for-bit identical with diff; re-ran tests and observed all three PASS. Evidence recorded in report.md under '### Adversarial Fidelity Proof'. Full Go unit suite (./smackerel.sh test unit --go) finished OK with no regression in any other package.

Clean simplify verdict for the BUG-016-W4 cache eviction change. Reviewed evictOneLocked for code reuse, quality, and efficiency opportunities; the two-loop structure (first loop returns early on expired hit; second loop linearly finds the latest-expiresAt victim) cannot be collapsed without losing the early-return optimization for the common expired-eviction case. No simplification worth applying.

Stabilize verdict for the BUG-016-W4 cache eviction change is stable: the delta only changes the in-process cache eviction policy of a single connector. No config, Docker, startup, service lifecycle, schema, persistent-state, or NATS subject changes. Memory behavior strictly improves under load (ephemeral entries are no longer permanently dropped after cache saturation). Shared live-stack stress readiness remains routed to BUG-031-005-stress-stack-health-readiness.

Security verdict for the BUG-016-W4 cache eviction change is secure: no secrets, auth, IDOR, injection, unsafe logging, privacy, SSRF, or data-leak risk introduced. The change does not touch HTTP request building, response parsing, location-name sanitization, or any auth-bearing surface. No new attack surface.

Validate-owned certification closure completed after green BUG-016-W4 build (check, lint, format --check), full Go unit suite (PASS), targeted weather cache tests (PASS), and adversarial fidelity cycle. Parent spec 016 state.json updated to append BUG-016-W4 to resolvedBugs[]; uservalidation.md updated with BUG-016-W4 entry. Scope 1 marked Done. Shared live-stack stress readiness remains routed to BUG-031-005.

Audit-owned certification review completed for BUG-016-W4: one completed scope, all 18 DoD items checked with executed-source evidence, required bugfix-fastlane phase provenance recorded, no framework-managed files edited, no other connector source touched, and adversarial fidelity proven by revert-and-restore cycle. Bug is closed.

executionHistory.summary: Created BUG-016-W4 bug.md and classified the finding as weather-connector owned with Finding IDs F1 and F2.
Root-cause triage authored in design.md; selected design helper signature and fallback branch confirmed.
design.md, spec.md, scopes.md, and scenario-manifest.json authored; SCN-BUG016W4-001..005 mapped to linked tests.

Applied the BUG-016-W4 production fix to internal/connector/weather/weather.go: renamed evictExpiredLocked to evictOneLocked, added the latest-expiresAt fallback eviction branch, and updated all three production callers.

Renamed TestEvictExpiredLocked to TestEvictOneLocked_PrefersExpired; updated TestCacheOverflow_AllValid for the new policy; added TestEviction_HistoricalDoesNotStarveEphemeral and TestEviction_LongestExpiryEvictedWhenFull. All five weather-cache tests PASS under go test -race -count=1 -v.

Proven adversarial fidelity: reverted the latest-expiresAt fallback branch in-place via scratch script, observed all three affected tests FAIL with their concrete BUG-016-W4 diagnostic strings, restored the fix from /tmp/weather-fixed.go and verified bit-for-bit identical via diff, re-ran the three tests and observed PASS. Full Go unit suite (./smackerel.sh test unit --go) finished OK with no regression in any other package.

Clean simplify verdict. The two-loop structure of evictOneLocked is intentional: the first loop early-returns on the common expired-eviction case; the second loop is only walked when no expired entries exist. Collapsing into a single pass would lose the early-return optimization.

Stabilize verdict: strictly improves under load. No config, Docker, startup, lifecycle, schema, or persistent-state changes. Memory bound preserved (maxCacheEntries=1024). Shared live-stack stress readiness routed to BUG-031-005.
Security verdict: secure. The change does not touch HTTP request building, response parsing, location-name sanitization, or any auth-bearing surface. No new attack surface introduced.

Validated BUG-016-W4 closure using the green check, lint, format --check, and full Go unit suite evidence. Updated parent spec 016 state.json resolvedBugs[] and uservalidation.md. Scope 1 marked Done. certification status promoted to done.

Audited BUG-016-W4 final certification packet: all 18 DoD items checked with executed-source evidence, Scope 1 is Done, required bugfix-fastlane phases have provenance, no framework-managed files were edited, no other connector source touched, and shared stress readiness is still routed to BUG-031-005.

completedScopes: Scope 1: Make weather cache eviction resilient to permanent historical entries

scopeProgress.name: Make weather cache eviction resilient to permanent historical entries

failures.summary: Historical entries with 100-year TTL crowd out ephemeral current/forecast entries from the bounded weather connector cache.
TestCacheOverflow_AllValid codified the broken policy and would block the fix.
